entries.title: torque
entries.subtitle: Reviewable Kubernetes deploy workflow CLI
entries.content:
torque plans, verifies, applies, captures, and observes Kubernetes deploy workflows.

Usage:
  torque <command> [flags]

Flags:
  -K, --context string                    Name of the kubeconfig context to use
      --kube-log-level int                Kubernetes client-go verbosity (klog -v); at
                                          >=6 enables HTTP request/response tracing;
                                          can also set TORQUE_KUBE_LOG_LEVEL
  -k, --kubeconfig string                 Path to the kubeconfig file to use for CLI
                                          requests
      --log-level string                  Log level for torque output (debug, info,
                                          warn, error) (default "info")
      --mirror-bus string                 Publish mirror payloads to a shared gRPC bus
                                          (torque-agent MirrorService)
      --no-color                          Disable colored output
      --profile string                    Execution profile: dev, ci, secure, or
                                          remote (sets sensible defaults for supported
                                          commands) (default "dev")
      --remote-agent string               Forward torque logs and deploy operations to
                                          a remote torque-agent gRPC endpoint
      --remote-tls                        Use TLS for remote gRPC endpoints (also via
                                          TORQUE_REMOTE_TLS=1)
      --remote-tls-ca string              CA bundle PEM file for remote gRPC TLS (also
                                          via TORQUE_REMOTE_TLS_CA)
      --remote-tls-client-cert string     Client certificate PEM file for remote gRPC
                                          mTLS (also via TORQUE_REMOTE_TLS_CLIENT_CERT)
      --remote-tls-client-key string      Client private key PEM file for remote gRPC
                                          mTLS (also via TORQUE_REMOTE_TLS_CLIENT_KEY)
      --remote-tls-insecure-skip-verify   Skip TLS verification for remote gRPC (also
                                          via TORQUE_REMOTE_TLS_INSECURE_SKIP_VERIFY=1)
      --remote-tls-server-name string     Override remote gRPC TLS server name (also
                                          via TORQUE_REMOTE_TLS_SERVER_NAME)
      --remote-token string               Authentication token for remote gRPC
                                          endpoints (also via TORQUE_REMOTE_TOKEN)
      --ui string[=":8080"]               Serve the interactive help UI at this
                                          address (e.g. :8080)
      --version                           Print version and exit
examples:
# Tail checkout pods in prod-payments and highlight errors
	  torque logs 'checkout-.*' --namespace prod-payments --highlight ERROR

# Initialize repo defaults
  torque init

# Launch the interactive help UI
  torque --help --ui

# Preview a Helm upgrade
  torque apply plan --chart ./chart --release foo

# Simulate live API behavior before applying
  torque apply simulate --chart ./chart --release foo --out ./torque-sim-proof

# Revert a release to the last known-good revision
  torque revert --release foo --namespace prod

# Turn failed apply proof into a repair plan
  torque repair --from ./apply-proof.json --chart ./chart

# Benchmark security detection with evidence
  torque security benchmark --corpus ./testdata/security --report benchmark.json

# Prove runtime drift from a simulation proof
  torque guardian diff --source ./torque-sim-proof --live --out drift-proof.json

# Synthesize and test runtime recurrence contracts
  torque contract synthesize --from incident-replay-proof --guardian drift-proof.json --out torque-contract.yaml

# Capture and replay incident evidence
  torque incident capture --release foo --namespace prod --since 1h --out incident.torque

# Apply chart changes
	torque apply --chart ./chart --release foo --namespace prod

# Launch the built-in interactive help UI
torque --help --ui

# Run the MCP bridge over stdio for an agent host
torque-mcp --stdio

# Bridge MCP calls to a remote torque-agent over gRPC
torque-mcp --stdio --remote-agent 127.0.0.1:7443 --remote-token "$TORQUE_REMOTE_TOKEN"

# Bridge MCP calls to an mTLS-protected torque-agent
torque-mcp --stdio --remote-agent torque-agent.prod.internal:7443 --remote-tls --remote-tls-ca /etc/torque/tls/ca.crt --remote-tls-client-cert /etc/torque/tls/client.crt --remote-tls-client-key /etc/torque/tls/client.key --remote-tls-server-name torque-agent.prod.internal --enable-write

# Ask MCP for a structured S3 cache plan instead of scraping BuildKit logs
printf '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"torque.cache.plan","arguments":{"contextDir":".","tags":["ghcr.io/acme/app:dev"],"changedPaths":["go.mod"],"s3Cache":"s3://acme-build-cache/torque/main","s3CacheRegion":"us-east-1"}}}\n' | torque-mcp --stdio

# Install durable gRPC and MCP services on a Linux systemd host
curl -fsSL https://ingresslabs.github.io/torque/install.sh | sh -s -- --mode systemd-daemon

entries.title: torque apply
entries.subtitle: Render and apply a Helm chart using upgrade --install
entries.content:
Render and apply a Helm chart using upgrade --install

Usage:
  torque apply [flags]

Flags:
      --atomic                             Rollback changes if the upgrade fails
                                           (default true)
      --auto-rollback                      Rollback and write proof when apply fails
                                           or a rollout SLO gate fails
      --capture string[="__auto__"]        Capture deploy events/logs/manifests to a
                                           SQLite database at this path
      --capture-tag stringArray            Tag the capture session (KEY=VALUE). Repeatable.
      --chart string                       Chart reference (path, repo/name, or OCI ref)
      --create-namespace                   Create the release namespace if it does not
                                           exist
      --drift-guard                        Fail if live cluster resources drift from
                                           the last applied Helm release state
      --drift-guard-mode string            Drift guard mode: last-applied (compare to
                                           current Helm release) or desired (compare
                                           to newly rendered manifest) (default
                                           "last-applied")
      --dry-run                            Render the chart without applying it
  -n, --namespace string                   Namespace for the Helm release (defaults to
                                           active context)
      --non-interactive                    Fail instead of prompting (requires --yes)
      --plan-server                        Use server-side dry-run to classify
                                           replacements (slower; requires RBAC)
      --predict                            Predict rollout risk, restarts, missing
                                           dependencies, quota pressure, and rollback
                                           confidence before applying
      --proof-bundle string[="__auto__"]   Write a predictive apply proof bundle JSON
                                           to this path (default:
                                           torque-proof-bundle-<release>-<timestamp>.json)
      --release string                     Helm release name
      --require-verified string            Require a matching verify report (JSON) for
                                           this exact render before applying
      --rollback-proof string              Write auto-rollback proof JSON to this path
                                           (default:
                                           torque-rollback-proof-<release>-<timestamp>.json)
      --secret-config string               Secrets provider config file (defaults to
                                           ~/.torque/config.yaml and repo .torque.yaml)
      --secret-provider string             Secret provider name for secret:// references
      --set stringArray                    Set values on the command line (key=val)
      --set-file stringArray               Set values from files (key=path)
      --set-string stringArray             Set STRING values on the command line
      --slo string                         Rollout SLO YAML for --auto-rollback gates
      --timeout duration                   Time to wait for any Kubernetes operation
                                           (default 5m0s)
      --ui string[=":8080"]                Serve the live deploy viewer at this
                                           address (e.g. :8080)
      --upgrade                            Only perform the upgrade path (skip install
                                           fallback)
  -f, --values strings                     Values files to apply (can be repeated)
  -v, --verbose                            Enable verbose logging (equivalent to
                                           --log-level=debug)
      --version string                     Chart version (default: latest)
      --wait                               Wait for resources to be ready (default true)
      --watch duration                     After a successful deploy, stream
                                           logs/events for this long (e.g. 2m)
      --ws-listen string                   Serve the raw deploy event stream over
                                           WebSocket at this address (e.g. :9086)
      --yes                                Alias for --auto-approve
examples:
# Apply a chart with prod values
  torque apply --chart ./charts/web --release web-prod --namespace prod -f values/prod.yaml

# Deploy a chart
torque apply --chart ./chart --release foo -n default

# Run the deploy viewer
torque apply --chart ./chart --release foo -n default --ui

# Predict rollout risk and write a proof bundle
torque apply --chart ./chart --release foo -n default --predict --proof-bundle ./apply-proof.json --capture ./apply.sqlite --yes

# Roll back automatically and write proof if apply or SLO gates fail
torque apply --chart ./chart --release foo -n default --auto-rollback --slo ./slo.yaml --capture ./apply.sqlite --yes

# Deploy with secret references
torque apply --chart ./chart --release foo -n default --secret-provider local

# Deploy with Vault-backed secrets
torque apply --chart ./chart --release foo -n default --secret-provider vault

entries.title: torque apply plan
entries.subtitle: Preview Helm release changes without applying them
entries.content:
Render the chart, diff it against live cluster resources, and summarize the net creates/updates/deletes before running torque apply.

Usage:
  torque apply plan [flags]

Flags:
      --baseline string             Write plan JSON baseline to this path
      --build-capture stringArray   Attach a torque build capture SQLite file for
                                    image provenance (repeatable)
      --chart string                Chart reference (path, repo/name, or OCI ref)
      --compare string              Plan artifact (path or URL) to embed for visualize
                                    comparisons
      --compare-exit                Exit non-zero when --compare-to detects
                                    regressions (default true)
      --compare-to string           Compare against a previous plan (path or URL) and
                                    report regressions
      --format string               Output format: text, json, yaml, markdown, or html
                                    (default "text")
      --github-comment              Render a GitHub PR comment markdown summary
      --include-crds                Render CRDs in addition to the main chart objects
  -n, --namespace string            Namespace for the Helm release (defaults to active
                                    context)
      --output string               Write the rendered plan to this path (HTML
                                    defaults to
                                    ./torque-deploy-plan-<release>-<timestamp>.html)
      --release string              Helm release name
      --secret-config string        Secrets provider config file (defaults to
                                    ~/.torque/config.yaml and repo .torque.yaml)
      --secret-provider string      Secret provider name for secret:// references
      --set stringArray             Set values on the command line (key=val)
      --set-file stringArray        Set values from files (key=path)
      --set-string stringArray      Set STRING values on the command line
  -f, --values strings              Values files to apply (can be repeated)
      --verify-report stringArray   Attach verifier JSON report to the plan artifact
                                    (repeatable)
      --version string              Chart version (default: latest)
      --visualize                   Render the interactive visualization
      --visualize-explain           Add an Explain Diff tab in --visualize output
examples:
# Preview a Helm upgrade
torque apply plan --chart ./chart --release foo -n default

# Write a GitHub PR comment summary
torque apply plan --chart ./chart --release foo -n default --github-comment --output plan.md

# Attach verifier and build evidence
torque apply plan --chart ./chart --release foo -n default --verify-report verify.json --build-capture ./build.sqlite --github-comment

# Render a shareable HTML visualization
torque apply plan --visualize --chart ./chart --release foo -n default

# Preview with secret references
torque apply plan --chart ./chart --release foo -n default --secret-provider local

# Preview with Vault-backed secrets
torque apply plan --chart ./chart --release foo -n default --secret-provider vault

# Compare against a saved baseline
torque apply plan --chart ./chart --release foo -n default --compare-to ./plan.json

# Write a baseline snapshot
torque apply plan --chart ./chart --release foo -n default --baseline ./plan.json

entries.title: torque apply simulate
entries.subtitle: Simulate a Helm apply against live API behavior and write a proof bundle
entries.content:
Render a Helm release, diff it against live state, run Kubernetes server-side dry-run checks, score rollout risk, and write a replayable simulation proof bundle without applying changes.

Usage:
  torque apply simulate [flags]

Flags:
      --chart string               Chart reference (path, repo/name, or OCI ref)
      --fail-on-blocked            Exit non-zero when the simulation proof is blocked
      --format string              Output format: text or json (default "text")
      --from-live                  Use live cluster state and server-side dry-run
                                   behavior (default true)
      --include-crds               Render CRDs in addition to the main chart objects
  -n, --namespace string           Namespace for the Helm release (defaults to active
                                   context)
      --out string                 Proof bundle directory (default:
                                   ./torque-sim-proof-<release>-<timestamp>)
      --release string             Helm release name
      --secret-config string       Secrets provider config file (defaults to
                                   ~/.torque/config.yaml and repo .torque.yaml)
      --secret-provider string     Secret provider name for secret:// references
      --security-evidence string   Existing verifier/security evidence directory to attach
      --set stringArray            Set values on the command line (key=val)
      --set-file stringArray       Set values from files (key=path)
      --set-string stringArray     Set STRING values on the command line
      --slo string                 Rollout SLO YAML to attach to the proof bundle
  -f, --values strings             Values files to apply (can be repeated)
      --version string             Chart version (default: latest)
examples:
# Simulate a Helm apply and write a proof bundle
torque apply simulate --chart ./chart --release foo -n default --out ./torque-sim-proof

# Attach security evidence and SLO gates
torque apply simulate --chart ./chart --release foo -n default --security-evidence ./torque-security-evidence --slo ./slo.yaml --out ./torque-sim-proof

# Fail CI when the API dry-run or quota proof blocks the release
torque apply simulate --chart ./chart --release foo -n default --out ./torque-sim-proof --fail-on-blocked

entries.title: torque build
entries.subtitle: Build container images with BuildKit
entries.content:
Build container images with BuildKit

Usage:
  torque build CONTEXT [flags]

Flags:
      --allow-network                  Allow network egress when --hermetic is enabled
      --allow-unpinned-bases           Allow unpinned base images (FROM without
                                       @sha256 digest) when --hermetic is enabled
      --attest-dir string              Write generated attestations (SBOM/provenance)
                                       to this directory as JSON files (implies --sbom
                                       and --provenance; requires OCI layout export)
      --authfile string                Path to the authentication file (Docker config.json)
      --build-arg stringArray          Add a build-time variable (KEY=VALUE)
      --builder string                 BuildKit address (override with
                                       TORQUE_BUILDKIT_HOST) (default
                                       "unix:///run/user/501/buildkit/buildkitd.sock")
      --cache-dir string               Local cache directory for BuildKit metadata
                                       (default
                                       "/Users/antonvkrylov/Library/Caches/torque/buildkit-cache")
      --cache-from stringArray         Cache import sources (comma-separated key=value
                                       pairs)
      --cache-intel                    Print a post-build cache summary (cache misses,
                                       slow steps, changed inputs) (default true)
      --cache-intel-format string      Cache intelligence output format: human or json
                                       (default "human")
      --cache-intel-output string      Write cache intelligence report to this path
                                       ('-' for stdout). Defaults to stderr in human mode.
      --cache-intel-top int            Max entries to show in the cache intelligence
                                       summary (default 10)
      --cache-to stringArray           Cache export destinations (comma-separated
                                       key=value pairs)
      --capture string[="__auto__"]    Capture build logs/events to a SQLite database
                                       at this path
      --capture-tag stringArray        Tag the capture session (KEY=VALUE). Repeatable.
      --compose-file stringArray       Compose file(s) to use when building compose
                                       projects
      --compose-parallelism int        Max parallel compose builds (default: NumCPU)
      --compose-profile stringArray    Compose profile(s) to enable
      --compose-project string         Override the compose project name
      --compose-service stringArray    Compose service(s) to build (default: all
                                       buildable services)
      --docker-context string          Docker context to use for buildx fallback
                                       (override with TORQUE_DOCKER_CONTEXT)
  -f, --file string                    Path to the Dockerfile (default: Dockerfile)
                                       (default "Dockerfile")
      --hermetic                       Enable hermetic/locked build mode (no network
                                       egress unless explicitly allowed; requires
                                       pinned base-image digests; records external fetches)
  -i, --interactive                    Drop into an interactive shell when a RUN step fails
      --interactive-shell string       Shell command to start when --interactive attaches
      --load                           Load the resulting image into the local
                                       container runtime (docker build --load)
      --locked                         Alias for --hermetic
      --logfile string                 Log to file instead of stdout/stderr
      --mode string                    Build mode: auto, dockerfile, or compose
      --no-cache                       Disable BuildKit cache usage
      --oci                            Intent: export OCI layout and write
                                       attestations (implies --attest-dir when unset)
      --output string                  Build output mode: auto (default), tty (live
                                       dashboard), or logs (streaming) (default "auto")
      --platform strings               Target platforms (comma-separated values like
                                       linux/amd64)
      --policy string                  Policy bundle path or https URL to evaluate
                                       before/after build (OPA/Rego).
      --policy-mode string             Policy enforcement mode: enforce or warn
                                       (default "enforce")
      --policy-report string           Write a machine-readable policy report JSON to
                                       this path (defaults to
                                       --attest-dir/torque-policy-report.json when
                                       --attest-dir is set)
      --provenance                     Generate a SLSA provenance attestation
                                       (in-toto) during the build
      --publish                        Intent: publish build (implies --push, and
                                       enables signing when combined with --sign)
      --push                           Push all tags to their registries after a
                                       successful build
  -q, --quiet                          Refrain from announcing build instructions and
                                       progress
      --rekor-url string               Override the Rekor transparency log URL used by
                                       cosign
      --remote string                  Remote builder address or 'auto' to provision
                                       ephemeral builder
      --remote-build string            Execute this build via a remote torque-agent
                                       gRPC endpoint (host:port)
      --rm                             Remove intermediate containers after a
                                       successful build (default true)
      --s3-cache string                Enable BuildKit S3 cache import/export at
                                       s3://BUCKET[/PREFIX]
      --s3-cache-endpoint-url string   Custom S3-compatible endpoint URL for --s3-cache
      --s3-cache-mode string           S3 cache export mode: min or max
      --s3-cache-name string           S3 cache manifest name (defaults to first tag
                                       or context name)
      --s3-cache-path-style            Use path-style addressing for --s3-cache
      --s3-cache-region string         AWS region for --s3-cache (defaults to
                                       AWS_REGION/AWS_DEFAULT_REGION when set)
      --sandbox                        Require executing the build inside the sandbox
                                       (fail if unavailable)
      --sandbox-bin string             Path to the sandbox runtime binary
      --sandbox-bind stringArray       Additional sandbox bind mounts (host:guest)
      --sandbox-bind-home              Bind-mount the current user's home directory
                                       into the sandbox (use with caution; prefer
                                       --authfile/--secret/--sandbox-bind)
      --sandbox-config string          Path to a sandbox runtime config file
      --sandbox-logs                   Stream sandbox runtime logs to stderr and the
                                       websocket mirror (when --ws-listen is set)
      --sandbox-probe-path string      Probe filesystem visibility before building by
                                       attempting to stat this host path
      --sandbox-workdir string         Working directory inside the sandbox (default:
                                       build context)
      --sbom                           Generate an SBOM attestation (in-toto) during
                                       the build
      --secret stringArray             Expose an environment variable as a BuildKit
                                       secret (NAME)
      --secrets string                 Secret-leak guardrails: warn (default), block,
                                       or off (default "warn")
      --secrets-config string          Secrets rule config file or https URL
                                       (YAML/JSON). When unset, built-in defaults apply.
      --secrets-report string          Write a machine-readable secrets report JSON to
                                       this path (defaults to
                                       --attest-dir/torque-secrets-report.json when
                                       --attest-dir is set)
      --secure                         Intent: secure build (implies
                                       hermetic+sandbox+attest+policy+secrets scan)
      --sign                           Sign pushed image tags with cosign after a
                                       successful build (requires --push)
      --sign-key string                cosign key reference for signing (e.g.
                                       awskms://..., gcpkms://..., azurekms://...)
  -t, --tag strings                    One or more image tags to apply to the result
      --tlog-upload string             Override cosign transparency log upload
                                       (true/false); default uses cosign's behavior
      --ws-listen string               Serve the raw BuildKit event stream over
                                       WebSocket at this address (e.g. :9085)
examples:
# Build the current directory
  torque build .

# Enforce an OPA/Rego policy bundle (writes JSON evidence to dist/attest)
  torque build . --attest-dir dist/attest --policy /path/to/policy --policy-mode enforce

# Auto-detect a compose project and build all services
  torque build ./testdata/build/compose

# Build from a compose file directly and limit parallelism
  torque build ./testdata/build/compose/docker-compose.yml --compose-parallelism 2

# Build with tags and push
  torque build . -f Dockerfile -t ghcr.io/acme/app:latest --push

# Build an image from a directory
torque build . --tag ghcr.io/acme/app:dev

# Build with a shared S3 cache
torque build . --tag ghcr.io/acme/app:dev --s3-cache s3://acme-build-cache/torque/main --s3-cache-region us-east-1

# Capture build provenance for apply plan
torque build . --tag ghcr.io/acme/app:dev --capture ./build.sqlite

# Share the build stream over WebSocket
torque build . --ws-listen :9085

entries.title: torque build login
entries.subtitle: Log in to a container registry
entries.content:
Log in to a container registry

Usage:
  torque build login [SERVER] [flags]

Flags:
  -p, --password string   Registry password or token (prefer --password-stdin)
      --password-stdin    Read the password/token from stdin
  -u, --username string   Registry username
examples:
# Login to Docker Hub (interactive prompts if flags are omitted)
  torque build login

# Login to GHCR using stdin (recommended)
  echo "$GITHUB_TOKEN" | torque build login ghcr.io --username your-github-username --password-stdin

entries.title: torque build logout
entries.subtitle: Log out of a container registry
entries.content:
Log out of a container registry

Usage:
  torque build logout [SERVER]
examples:
# Logout of Docker Hub
  torque build logout

# Logout of a specific registry
  torque build logout ghcr.io

entries.title: torque build sandbox
entries.subtitle: Sandbox tooling for torque build
entries.content:
Sandbox tooling for torque build

Usage:
  torque build sandbox

entries.title: torque build sandbox doctor
entries.subtitle: Diagnose nsjail sandbox readiness for torque build
entries.content:
Diagnose nsjail sandbox readiness for torque build

Usage:
  torque build sandbox doctor [flags]

Flags:
      --context string   Build context directory to use when probing (default: cwd)

entries.title: torque contract
entries.subtitle: Synthesize and test runtime contracts from proof
entries.content:
Runtime Contract turns Guardian drift proof and Incident replay proof into observe-only recurrence rules, machine-checkable test proof, and PR-ready artifacts.

Usage:
  torque contract
examples:
# Synthesize recurrence rules from Incident and Guardian proof
torque contract synthesize --from incident-replay-proof --guardian drift-proof.json --out torque-contract.yaml

# Test fresh proof against the RuntimeContract
torque contract test --contract torque-contract.yaml --from incident-replay-proof --guardian drift-proof.json --out contract-proof.json

# Generate PR artifacts from contract proof
torque contract pr --contract torque-contract.yaml --proof contract-proof.json --branch add/api-runtime-contract

entries.title: torque contract pr
entries.subtitle: Generate PR-ready artifacts from a RuntimeContract proof
entries.content:
Generate PR-ready artifacts from a RuntimeContract proof

Usage:
  torque contract pr [flags]

Flags:
      --branch string     Suggested contract branch name
      --contract string   RuntimeContract YAML/JSON file
      --format string     Output format: text or json (default "text")
      --out string        Directory for PR artifacts (default: ./fix beside --proof)
      --proof string      RuntimeContract proof JSON from torque contract test
examples:
# Generate patch and PR body from contract proof
torque contract pr --contract torque-contract.yaml --proof contract-proof.json --branch add/api-runtime-contract

entries.title: torque contract synthesize
entries.subtitle: Synthesize a RuntimeContract from proof evidence
entries.content:
Synthesize a RuntimeContract from proof evidence

Usage:
  torque contract synthesize [flags]

Flags:
      --format string     Output format: text, json, or yaml (default "text")
      --from string       Incident bundle, root cause JSON, or incident replay proof
                          directory
      --guardian string   Guardian drift proof JSON or runtime proof directory
      --out string        Write RuntimeContract YAML/JSON to this path
examples:
# Write a RuntimeContract from combined proof
torque contract synthesize --from incident-replay-proof --guardian drift-proof.json --out torque-contract.yaml

# Print the synthesized contract as YAML
torque contract synthesize --from incident-replay-proof --guardian drift-proof.json --format yaml

entries.title: torque contract test
entries.subtitle: Test a RuntimeContract against proof evidence
entries.content:
Test a RuntimeContract against proof evidence

Usage:
  torque contract test [flags]

Flags:
      --contract string   RuntimeContract YAML/JSON file
      --fail-on-blocked   Exit non-zero when the contract test fails
      --format string     Output format: text or json (default "text")
      --from string       Incident bundle, root cause JSON, or incident replay proof
                          directory
      --guardian string   Guardian drift proof JSON or runtime proof directory
      --out string        Write contract test proof JSON to this path
examples:
# Write machine-checkable contract test proof
torque contract test --contract torque-contract.yaml --from incident-replay-proof --guardian drift-proof.json --out contract-proof.json

# Fail CI when recurrence rules are violated
torque contract test --contract torque-contract.yaml --from incident-replay-proof --guardian drift-proof.json --fail-on-blocked

entries.title: torque delete
entries.subtitle: Delete a Helm release and its resources
entries.content:
Delete a Helm release and its resources

Usage:
  torque delete [flags]

Flags:
      --capture string[="__auto__"]   Capture destroy events/logs to a SQLite database
                                      at this path
      --capture-tag stringArray       Tag the capture session (KEY=VALUE). Repeatable.
      --disable-hooks                 Disable Helm hooks while destroying the release
      --dry-run                       Simulate destroy without removing resources
      --force                         Force uninstall even if Kubernetes resources are
                                      in a bad state
      --keep-history                  Retain release history (equivalent to helm
                                      uninstall --keep-history)
  -n, --namespace string              Namespace for the Helm release (defaults to
                                      active context)
      --non-interactive               Fail instead of prompting (requires --yes)
      --release string                Helm release name
      --timeout duration              How long to wait for resource deletions (default
                                      5m0s)
      --ui string[=":8080"]           Serve the destroy viewer at this address (e.g. :8080)
  -v, --verbose                       Enable verbose logging (equivalent to
                                      --log-level=debug)
      --wait                          Wait for resources to be deleted
      --ws-listen string              Serve the destroy event stream over WebSocket
                                      (e.g. :9087)
      --yes                           Alias for --auto-approve
examples:
# Delete a release but keep its history
  torque delete --release web-prod --namespace prod --keep-history

# Delete a release
torque delete --release foo -n default

# Run the destroy viewer
torque delete --release foo -n default --ui

entries.title: torque env
entries.subtitle: Show environment variables used by torque
entries.content:
Show environment variables used by torque

Usage:
  torque env [flags]

Flags:
      --all               Include internal/legacy variables
      --category string   Filter to a category (case-insensitive)
      --format string     Output format: table, json, yaml (default "table")
      --match string      Filter by substring match against name/category/description
      --set               Show only variables with a non-empty value
examples:
# Show env var reference (machine-readable)
torque env --format json

entries.title: torque explain
entries.subtitle: Explain a captured torque run
entries.content:
Explain a captured torque deploy, build, log, or stack run from a portable SQLite evidence file.

Usage:
  torque explain CAPTURE.sqlite [flags]

Flags:
      --format string    Output format: text, markdown, or json (default "text")
      --max-hints int    Maximum failure hints to print per session (default 8)
      --session string   Explain only this session_id or run_id
examples:
# Explain a deploy capture
  torque explain ./apply.sqlite

# Emit machine-readable evidence summary
  torque explain ./stack.sqlite --format json

# Explain a deploy capture
torque explain ./apply.sqlite

# Explain a stack capture as JSON
torque explain ./stack.sqlite --format json

# Write a Markdown explanation for CI logs or PR comments
torque explain ./apply.sqlite --format markdown

# Explain a specific captured session
torque explain ./apply.sqlite --session <session-id>

entries.title: torque guardian
entries.subtitle: Observe runtime drift and produce repair evidence
entries.content:
Torque Guardian connects simulation proof, live runtime state, Kubernetes events, managed fields, secret-boundary checks, and PR-ready repair artifacts in observe-only mode.

Usage:
  torque guardian
examples:
# Install observe-only Guardian RBAC and config
torque guardian install --namespace torque-system --mode observe

# Compare a simulation proof against live runtime state
torque guardian diff --source ./torque-sim-proof --live --out drift-proof.json

# Generate PR artifacts from a drift proof
torque guardian pr --from drift-proof.json --branch fix/runtime-drift

entries.title: torque guardian diff
entries.subtitle: Compare simulation proof with live runtime state
entries.content:
Compare simulation proof with live runtime state

Usage:
  torque guardian diff [flags]

Flags:
      --format string      Output format: text or json (default "text")
      --live               Compare against live Kubernetes objects (default true)
  -n, --namespace string   Namespace override for namespaced resources
      --out string         Write drift proof JSON or proof directory
      --since string       Runtime event window, for example 24h or 30m (default "24h")
      --source string      Simulation proof directory from torque apply simulate
examples:
# Prove drift from simulation proof to live objects
torque guardian diff --source ./torque-sim-proof --live --out drift-proof.json

# Write the full runtime proof bundle directory
torque guardian diff --source ./torque-sim-proof --live --out ./torque-runtime-proof

entries.title: torque guardian install
entries.subtitle: Install observe-only Guardian RBAC and config
entries.content:
Install observe-only Guardian RBAC and config

Usage:
  torque guardian install [flags]

Flags:
      --dry-run            Print the install manifest without applying it
      --mode string        Guardian mode (observe) (default "observe")
      --namespace string   Namespace for Guardian observe config (default "torque-system")
      --output string      Write install manifest to this path instead of applying it
examples:
# Install observe-only Guardian RBAC
torque guardian install --namespace torque-system --mode observe

# Review the manifest without applying it
torque guardian install --namespace torque-system --mode observe --dry-run

entries.title: torque guardian pr
entries.subtitle: Generate PR-ready artifacts from Guardian drift proof
entries.content:
Generate PR-ready artifacts from Guardian drift proof

Usage:
  torque guardian pr [flags]

Flags:
      --branch string   Suggested repair branch name
      --format string   Output format: text or json (default "text")
      --from string     Guardian drift proof JSON or runtime proof directory
      --out string      Directory for fix artifacts (default: ./fix beside the proof)
examples:
# Generate patch and PR body from a drift proof
torque guardian pr --from drift-proof.json --branch fix/runtime-drift

# Generate fix artifacts beside a runtime proof bundle
torque guardian pr --from ./torque-runtime-proof

entries.title: torque guardian report
entries.subtitle: Write a runtime proof from live events
entries.content:
Write a runtime proof from live events

Usage:
  torque guardian report [flags]

Flags:
      --all-namespaces     Inspect events across all namespaces
      --format string      Output format: text or json (default "text")
  -n, --namespace string   Namespace to inspect (defaults to active context)
      --out string         Write runtime proof JSON to this path
      --since string       Runtime event window, for example 24h or 30m (default "24h")
examples:
# Write a 24-hour runtime event proof
torque guardian report --since 24h --out runtime-proof.json

# Inspect all namespaces and print JSON
torque guardian report --since 30m --all-namespaces --format json

entries.title: torque incident
entries.subtitle: Capture and replay observe-only incident evidence
entries.content:
Torque Incident captures release runtime evidence, replays it as a portable lab proof, explains likely root cause, and writes PR-ready artifacts without mutating the cluster.

Usage:
  torque incident
examples:
# Capture observe-only incident evidence
torque incident capture --release api -n prod --since 1h --out incident.torque

# Replay the capture as a lab proof
torque incident replay incident.torque --lab k3s --out incident-replay-proof

# Generate PR artifacts from root cause
torque incident pr --from incident-replay-proof --branch fix/api-incident

entries.title: torque incident capture
entries.subtitle: Capture observe-only incident evidence for a release
entries.content:
Capture observe-only incident evidence for a release

Usage:
  torque incident capture [flags]

Flags:
      --format string      Output format: text or json (default "text")
      --log-tail int       Log lines per container to include (0 disables logs)
                           (default 80)
  -n, --namespace string   Release namespace (defaults to active context)
      --out string         Write incident bundle JSON to this path
      --release string     Helm release name to capture
      --since string       Incident evidence window, for example 1h or 30m (default "1h")
examples:
# Capture a release incident bundle
torque incident capture --release api -n prod --since 1h --out incident.torque

# Print capture JSON and skip pod logs
torque incident capture --release api -n prod --log-tail 0 --format json

entries.title: torque incident explain
entries.subtitle: Explain root cause from incident proof
entries.content:
Explain root cause from incident proof

Usage:
  torque incident explain [flags]

Flags:
      --format string   Output format: text or json (default "text")
      --from string     Incident bundle or replay proof directory
      --out string      Write root cause JSON to this path
examples:
# Write root-cause JSON from replay proof
torque incident explain --from incident-replay-proof --out root-cause.json

entries.title: torque incident pr
entries.subtitle: Generate PR-ready artifacts from incident root cause
entries.content:
Generate PR-ready artifacts from incident root cause

Usage:
  torque incident pr [flags]

Flags:
      --branch string   Suggested repair branch name
      --format string   Output format: text or json (default "text")
      --from string     Root cause JSON, incident bundle, or replay proof directory
      --out string      Directory for fix artifacts (default: ./fix beside --from)
examples:
# Generate patch and PR body from root cause
torque incident pr --from root-cause.json --branch fix/api-incident

# Generate fix artifacts beside replay proof
torque incident pr --from incident-replay-proof

entries.title: torque incident replay
entries.subtitle: Replay an incident bundle as a lab proof
entries.content:
Replay an incident bundle as a lab proof

Usage:
  torque incident replay <incident-bundle> [flags]

Flags:
      --fail-on-blocked   Exit non-zero when replayed incident remains blocked
      --format string     Output format: text or json (default "text")
      --lab string        Replay lab profile label (default "k3s")
      --out string        Write incident replay proof directory
examples:
# Validate an incident bundle in the k3s lab profile
torque incident replay incident.torque --lab k3s --out incident-replay-proof

# Fail CI when replayed evidence remains blocked
torque incident replay incident.torque --lab k3s --fail-on-blocked

entries.title: torque init
entries.subtitle: Initialize a repo with torque defaults
entries.content:
Creates a repo-local .torque.yaml with sane defaults and prints a short
getting-started checklist.

Usage:
  torque init [path] [flags]

Flags:
      --apply-plan string         Apply a previously generated init plan (path or -
                                  for stdin)
      --dry-run                   Print the .torque.yaml that would be written
      --force                     Overwrite an existing .torque.yaml
      --gitignore                 Add the secrets file to .gitignore
      --gitignore-config          Also add .torque.yaml to .gitignore
      --interactive               Run an interactive setup wizard
      --layout                    Create chart/ and values/ directories
      --merge                     Merge defaults into an existing .torque.yaml
      --output string             Output format: text or json (default "text")
      --plan                      Output a replayable init plan as JSON (no changes
                                  applied)
      --plan-output string        Write plan JSON to a file instead of stdout
      --preset string             Opinionated init preset: dev, ci, prod
      --secrets-file string       Local secrets file path to reference in .torque.yaml
                                  (default "./secrets.dev.yaml")
      --secrets-provider string   Secrets provider to scaffold (local, vault, aws,
                                  k8s) (default "local")
      --show-diff                 Show a diff when merging into an existing .torque.yaml
      --stack                     Create a minimal stack.yaml in the repo root
      --template string           Apply a config template (name, path, or URL).
                                  Built-ins: platform, secure
      --validate                  Validate the generated config for common mistakes
                                  (default true)
      --values                    Create values/dev.yaml and values/prod.yaml
examples:
# Create a repo-local .torque.yaml
torque init

# Preview the config without writing
torque init --dry-run

# Run the interactive wizard
torque init --interactive

# Use an opinionated preset
torque init --preset prod

# Apply a built-in template
torque init --template platform

# Apply a template from a URL
torque init --template https://example.com/torque-init.yaml

# Merge defaults into an existing config
torque init --merge

# Scaffold chart/ and values/ plus gitignore
torque init --layout --gitignore

# Scaffold Vault-backed secrets
torque init --secrets-provider vault

# Emit JSON for automation
torque init --output json --dry-run

# Write a replayable init plan
torque init --plan --plan-output .torque/init-plan.json

# Apply a saved init plan
torque init --apply-plan .torque/init-plan.json

# Initialize another path
torque init ./services/api

# Overwrite existing config
torque init --force

entries.title: torque init from-cluster
entries.subtitle: Generate a starter stack.yaml from an existing cluster
entries.content:
Inspects the current cluster for Helm releases, namespaces, GitOps resources,
local Helmfile files, and current workloads, then writes a starter stack.yaml.

The generated stack is executable for discovered Helm releases. Argo CD, Flux,
Helmfile, and non-Helm workloads are preserved as adoption notes so teams can
move incrementally instead of rewriting their cluster before torque is useful.

Usage:
  torque init from-cluster [path] [flags]

Flags:
  -A, --all-namespaces          Inspect every namespace
      --charts-dir string       Directory for --write-charts output (default
                                "charts/adopted")
      --cluster-name string     Cluster target name to write in stack defaults
      --dry-run                 Print the generated stack.yaml without writing files
      --force                   Overwrite existing stack.yaml and generated values files
      --gitops                  Discover Argo CD and Flux resources (default true)
      --helm                    Discover Helm releases (default true)
      --include-system          Include kube-* namespaces when using --all-namespaces
      --name string             Stack name to write
  -n, --namespace stringArray   Namespace to inspect (repeatable or comma-separated;
                                defaults to current namespace)
      --output string           Output stack path, or - for stdout (default "stack.yaml")
      --values-dir string       Directory for --write-values output (default
                                "values/adopted")
      --workloads               Discover current Kubernetes workloads (default true)
      --write-charts            Export installed Helm chart archives and reference
                                them from stack.yaml (default true)
      --write-values            Write current Helm values and reference them from
                                stack.yaml
examples:
# Generate stack.yaml from the active namespace
  torque init from-cluster

# Inspect every non-system namespace
  torque init from-cluster --all-namespaces

# Preview without writing
  torque init from-cluster --all-namespaces --dry-run

# Export installed charts and current Helm values
  torque init from-cluster --write-values

# Generate stack.yaml from the active namespace
torque init from-cluster

# Preview adoption across all non-system namespaces
torque init from-cluster --all-namespaces --dry-run

# Export installed charts and current Helm values
torque init from-cluster --all-namespaces --write-values

# Adopt a specific namespace into another file
torque init from-cluster -n prod --output stacks/prod.yaml

entries.title: torque lint
entries.subtitle: Examine a chart for possible issues
entries.content:
Examine a chart for possible issues

Usage:
  torque lint [CHART]... [flags]

Flags:
      --kube-version string      Kubernetes version used for capabilities and
                                 deprecation checks
  -n, --namespace string         Namespace used for template rendering (default:
                                 current context)
      --quiet                    Print only warnings and errors
      --set stringArray          Set values on the command line (key=val)
      --set-file stringArray     Set values from files (key=path)
      --set-string stringArray   Set STRING values on the command line
      --skip-schema-validation   Disable JSON schema validation
      --strict                   Fail on lint warnings
  -f, --values strings           Values files to apply (can be repeated)
      --with-subcharts           Lint dependent charts under charts/
examples:
# Lint the current chart directory
  torque lint

# Lint a specific chart with values
  torque lint ./chart -f values/prod.yaml --strict

entries.title: torque list
entries.subtitle: List Helm releases
entries.content:
List Helm releases

Usage:
  torque list [flags]

Flags:
  -a, --all                  Show all releases without any filter applied
  -A, --all-namespaces       List releases across all namespaces
  -d, --date                 Sort by release date
      --deployed             Show deployed releases. If no other is specified, this
                             will be automatically enabled
      --failed               Show failed releases
  -f, --filter string        A regular expression (Perl compatible) to filter releases
                             by name
      --format string        Output format: table, json, or yaml (default "table")
  -m, --max int              Maximum number of releases to fetch (default 256)
  -n, --namespace string     Namespace scope for listing releases (defaults to active
                             context)
      --no-headers           Don't print headers when using the default output format
      --offset int           Next release index in the list, used to offset from start
      --pending              Show pending releases
  -r, --reverse              Reverse the sort order
  -l, --selector string      Label selector to filter releases by label query (works
                             only for secret/configmap backends)
  -q, --short                Output short (quiet) listing format
      --superseded           Show superseded releases
      --time-format string   Format time using Go time layout. Example: --time-format
                             "2006-01-02 15:04:05Z0700"
      --uninstalled          Show uninstalled releases (if uninstall --keep-history
                             was used)
      --uninstalling         Show releases that are currently being uninstalled
examples:
# List releases in the current namespace
  torque list

# List across all namespaces
  torque list -A

# Emit structured output
  torque list --format json

entries.title: torque logs
entries.subtitle: Tail Kubernetes pod logs
entries.content:
Stream pod logs with torque's high-performance tailer. Accepts the same query/flag set as the legacy torque entrypoint.

Usage:
  torque logs [POD_QUERY] [flags]

Flags:
  -A, --all-namespaces                If present, tail across all namespaces
                                      (overrides --namespace)
      --capture string[="__auto__"]   Capture logs to a SQLite database at this path
      --capture-tag stringArray       Tag the capture session (KEY=VALUE). Repeatable.
  -m, --color string                  Force set color output. 'auto': colorize if tty
                                      attached, 'always': always colorize, 'never':
                                      never colorize (default "auto")
      --condition stringArray         Filter pods by condition, e.g. ready=false
      --config string                 Path to stack.yaml (used with --deps)
  -c, --container strings             Regex filter for container names (repeat to OR
                                      multiple)
      --container-colors strings      Comma-separated SGR color codes used to color
                                      container names
      --deploy-mode string            When using deploy/<name>, pick which replica
                                      sets to follow: active, stable, canary,
                                      stable+canary (default "active")
      --deploy-pin string             Deprecated: use --deploy-mode. When using
                                      deploy/<name>, pin replica sets in the selection
                                      (comma-separated: stable,canary)
      --deploy-prune-grace duration   When using deploy/<name>, keep tailed pods for
                                      this long after they fall out of the selected
                                      replica sets (default 15s)
      --deploy-refresh duration       When using deploy/<name>, refresh deployment
                                      selection at this interval (fallback when watch
                                      is unavailable) (default 2s)
      --deps                          Include logs from dependencies defined in stack.yaml
  -d, --diff-container                Display different colors for different containers
  -e, --events                        Include Kubernetes events that match the pod selector
  -O, --events-only                   Only stream matching events (implies --events)
  -x, --exclude string                Regex to skip log lines that match
  -C, --exclude-container strings     Regex for container names to exclude
      --exclude-pod strings           Regex for pod names to exclude
      --field-selector string         Field selector to filter pods (e.g.
                                      spec.nodeName=kind-control-plane)
      --filter string                 Filter JSON logs by key=value (e.g. level=error,
                                      status=500)
  -f, --follow                        Follow log output (default true)
  -H, --highlight stringArray         Log lines to highlight (regular expression)
  -j, --json                          Emit raw JSON log lines without decoration
  -n, --namespace strings             Kubernetes namespace to use. Defaults to the
                                      context namespace; repeat or use comma-separated
                                      values for multiple.
      --no-follow                     Alias for --follow=false
  -P, --no-prefix                     Disable default prefix (overrides template to
                                      just message)
      --node-log strings              Specific node/system log files (relative to
                                      /var/log) to stream via the kubelet proxy;
                                      repeat for multiple
      --node-log-all                  Stream node logs from every node instead of only
                                      those hosting matched pods
      --node-log-only                 Suppress pod logs and stream only node/system logs
      --node-logs                     Also stream node/system logs (defaults to
                                      kubelet.log) from nodes hosting matched pods
      --only-log-lines                Print only the log message body (no timestamps
                                      or prefixes)
  -o, --output string                 Specify predefined template: default, raw, json,
                                      extjson, ppextjson (default "default")
      --plain                         Shorthand for --only-log-lines and --no-prefix
  -g, --pod-colors strings            Comma-separated SGR color codes used to color
                                      pod names (e.g. "91,92,93")
  -l, --selector string               Label selector to filter pods
  -s, --since string[="0s"]           Return logs newer than a relative duration like
                                      5s, 2m, or 3h
      --stdin                         Read log lines from STDIN instead of Kubernetes
  -t, --tail int[=10]                 Number of historic log lines to show, -1 for all
                                      available (default 10)
  -p, --template string               Go template for log lines; available fields:
                                      Timestamp, Namespace, PodName, ContainerName,
                                      Message, Raw (default "[{{.Timestamp}}]
                                      {{.PodDisplay}} {{.ContainerTag}} {{.Message}}")
      --template-file string          Path to a Go template file for log output
  -F, --timestamp-format string       Go time format string for timestamps (use
                                      "youtube" for H:MM:SS) (default "youtube")
  -T, --timestamps                    Show timestamps in output (default true)
      --timezone string               IANA timezone name used when rendering
                                      timestamps (e.g. Asia/Tokyo)
      --ws-listen string              Expose a raw WebSocket log feed at this address
                                      (e.g. :9090)
examples:
# Tail pods matching a regex in a namespace
torque logs 'checkout-.*' -n prod-payments

# Highlight errors
torque logs 'checkout-.*' -n prod-payments --highlight ERROR

entries.title: torque repair
entries.subtitle: Turn failed apply proof into a chart repair plan
entries.content:
Read a Torque apply or simulation proof bundle, diagnose likely rollout root cause, and optionally write PR-ready chart repair files.

Usage:
  torque repair [flags]

Flags:
      --apply             Write repair files into the chart
      --branch string     Repair branch name to create when --apply is used in a clean
                          git worktree
      --chart string      Chart directory to patch
      --format string     Output format: text, json, or markdown (default "text")
      --from string       Apply proof bundle JSON to diagnose
      --non-interactive   Fail instead of prompting (requires --yes)
      --pr-body string    Write a Markdown PR body to this path
      --yes               Auto-approve repair file writes
examples:
# Diagnose a failed apply proof bundle
torque repair --from ./apply-proof.json --chart ./chart

# Diagnose a simulation proof bundle through the fix alias
torque fix --from ./torque-sim-proof --chart ./chart

# Write chart repair files and a PR body
torque repair --from ./apply-proof.json --chart ./chart --branch fix/foo-rollout --apply --pr-body ./repair-pr.md --yes

entries.title: torque replay
entries.subtitle: Replay and validate a Torque proof bundle
entries.content:
Validate a Torque simulation or apply proof bundle and report whether its evidence can be replayed in a lab workflow.

Usage:
  torque replay <proof-bundle> [flags]

Flags:
      --fail-on-blocked   Exit non-zero when the replayed proof is blocked
      --format string     Output format: text or json (default "text")
      --lab string        Replay lab profile label (default "k3s")
examples:
# Validate a simulation proof bundle in the k3s lab profile
torque replay ./torque-sim-proof --lab k3s

# Emit replay validation as JSON
torque replay ./torque-sim-proof --lab k3s --format json

# Fail CI when the replayed proof is blocked
torque replay ./torque-sim-proof --lab k3s --fail-on-blocked

entries.title: torque revert
entries.subtitle: Revert a Helm release to a previous revision
entries.content:
Revert a Helm release to a previous revision

Usage:
  torque revert [flags]

Flags:
      --dry-run            Show the target revision and exit without changing the cluster
  -n, --namespace string   Namespace for the Helm release (defaults to active context)
      --non-interactive    Fail instead of prompting (requires --yes)
      --release string     Helm release name
      --revision int       Target revision to revert to (default: last known-good)
      --timeout duration   How long to wait for the rollback (default 5m0s)
  -v, --verbose            Enable verbose logging (equivalent to --log-level=debug)
      --wait               Wait for resources to become ready (default true)
      --yes                Auto-approve confirmation prompts
examples:
# Revert a release to the last known-good revision
torque revert --release foo -n default

entries.title: torque secrets
entries.subtitle: Inspect and validate deploy-time secret providers
entries.content:
Inspect and validate deploy-time secret providers

Usage:
  torque secrets
examples:
# Validate a secret reference
torque secrets test --secret-provider vault --ref secret://vault/app/db#password

# List secrets under a provider prefix
torque secrets list --secret-provider local --path app --format json

# Discover secret refs across the repo
torque secrets discover --scope repo

# Discover secret refs for a chart
torque secrets discover --scope chart --chart ./chart --values values/dev.yaml

# Discover secret refs for a stack
torque secrets discover --scope stack --config ./stacks/prod

# Scan source files for secret-like values
torque secrets scan --scope repo --report secrets.json --mode block

# Scan source files and include a redacted secret flow graph
torque secrets scan --scope repo --report secrets.json --mode block --flow-graph

# Scan rendered manifests and allow Kubernetes Secret boundaries
torque secrets scan --scope render --manifest ./rendered.yaml --report render-secrets.json --mode block --flow-graph

entries.title: torque secrets discover
entries.subtitle: Scan charts and values for secret:// references
entries.content:
Scan charts and values for secret:// references

Usage:
  torque secrets discover [flags]

Flags:
      --chart string             Chart path or reference (scope=chart)
      --chart-repo string        Chart repository URL (scope=chart)
      --chart-version string     Chart version (scope=chart)
      --config string            Stack root directory or stack.yaml (scope=stack)
      --include-orphaned         Include invalid or unconfigured secret references
      --include-unused           Include configured but unused secret providers
      --output string            Output format: table or json (default "table")
      --root string              Repo root (scope=repo) or stack root when --config is
                                 unset (default ".")
      --scope string             Discovery scope: repo, chart, or stack (default "repo")
      --secret-config string     Secrets provider config file (defaults to
                                 ~/.torque/config.yaml and repo .torque.yaml)
      --secret-provider string   Default secret provider name for secret:// references
      --set strings              Set values on the command line (scope=chart)
      --set-file strings         Set values from files on the command line (scope=chart)
      --set-string strings       Set string values on the command line (scope=chart)
      --values strings           Values file(s) to include (scope=chart)

entries.title: torque secrets exec
entries.subtitle: Execute a command with secrets injected as environment variables
entries.content:
Injects all keys from a Kubernetes Secret as environment variables into a local command.
Keys are automatically capitalized and sanitized (e.g. "db.password" -> "DB_PASSWORD").

Example:
  torque secrets exec my-db-secret -- ./start-app

Usage:
  torque secrets exec [SECRET_NAME] -- [COMMAND]... [flags]

Flags:
  -n, --namespace string   Kubernetes namespace

entries.title: torque secrets list
entries.subtitle: List secrets under a provider/path
entries.content:
List secrets under a provider/path

Usage:
  torque secrets list [flags]

Flags:
      --format string            Output format: text or json (default "text")
      --path string              Secret path to list (default: provider root)
      --secret-config string     Secrets provider config file (defaults to
                                 ~/.torque/config.yaml and repo .torque.yaml)
      --secret-provider string   Secret provider name

entries.title: torque secrets scan
entries.subtitle: Scan source, rendered manifests, or artifacts for secret evidence
entries.content:
Scan source files, rendered Kubernetes manifests, or text artifacts for secret-like values and write a redacted evidence-first report.

Usage:
  torque secrets scan [ARTIFACT] [flags]

Flags:
      --chart string              Helm chart path for --scope render
      --evaluated-at string       Override evaluation time (RFC3339) for deterministic
                                  reports/tests
      --fail-on string            Fail threshold: info|low|medium|high|critical
                                  (default "high")
      --flow-graph                Add a redacted secret flow graph to the JSON report
      --format string             Output format: table or json (default "table")
      --manifest string           Rendered manifest path for --scope render
      --mode string               Scan mode: warn|block|off (default "warn")
  -n, --namespace string          Namespace for --scope render
      --release string            Helm release name for --scope render
      --report string             Write JSON report to this path
      --root string               Root directory or artifact path to scan (default ".")
      --scope string              Scan scope: repo, render, build, or artifact
                                  (default "repo")
      --security-profile string   Security profile to apply (default or enterprise)
      --set strings               Set value(s) for --scope render chart mode
      --values strings            Values file(s) for --scope render chart mode

entries.title: torque secrets test
entries.subtitle: Validate access to a secret reference
entries.content:
Validate access to a secret reference

Usage:
  torque secrets test [flags]

Flags:
      --key string               Secret key (optional when not using --ref)
      --path string              Secret path (when not using --ref)
      --ref string               Secret reference (secret://provider/path#key)
      --secret-config string     Secrets provider config file (defaults to
                                 ~/.torque/config.yaml and repo .torque.yaml)
      --secret-provider string   Secret provider name for secret:// references

entries.title: torque security
entries.subtitle: Run security evidence workflows
entries.content:
Run security evidence workflows such as benchmark corpus evaluation for secret detection, redaction, and Kubernetes boundary proofs.

Usage:
  torque security
examples:
# Benchmark secret detection, redaction, flow graph, and boundary evidence
torque security benchmark --corpus ./testdata/security --report benchmark.json

entries.title: torque security benchmark
entries.subtitle: Benchmark secret detection, redaction, and boundary evidence
entries.content:
Benchmark secret detection against a corpus and publish recall, precision, false-positive, runtime, redaction, flow-graph, and Kubernetes boundary metrics.

Usage:
  torque security benchmark [flags]

Flags:
      --corpus string              Security benchmark corpus directory (default
                                   "./testdata/security")
      --evaluated-at string        Override evaluation time (RFC3339) for
                                   deterministic reports/tests
      --format string              Output format: table or json (default "table")
      --live-confirm               Confirm that --live-k3s-boundary-matrix may create
                                   and delete its namespace
      --live-k3s-boundary-matrix   Run the live k3s boundary matrix probe against a
                                   temporary namespace
      --live-namespace string      Namespace for --live-k3s-boundary-matrix (default:
                                   generated)
      --report string              Write benchmark JSON report to this path
examples:
# Publish corpus-backed security metrics
torque security benchmark --corpus ./testdata/security --report benchmark.json

# Include the live k3s boundary matrix probe
torque security benchmark --corpus ./testdata/security --report benchmark.json --live-k3s-boundary-matrix --live-confirm --kubeconfig ~/.kube/k3s.yaml

entries.title: torque ship
entries.subtitle: Build, verify, plan, apply, capture, and explain one release
entries.content:
Run the focused build-to-deploy workflow and write a portable evidence directory for the release.

Usage:
  torque ship [flags]

Flags:
      --allow-network                  Allow network egress when --hermetic is enabled
      --allow-unpinned-bases           Allow unpinned base images when --hermetic is
                                       enabled
      --apply-capture string           Apply capture path (defaults inside --evidence-dir)
      --atomic                         Rollback changes if the upgrade fails (default true)
      --attest                         Write SBOM/provenance attestations into the
                                       evidence directory (default true)
      --attest-dir string              Write generated attestations to this directory
                                       (defaults inside --evidence-dir)
      --authfile string                Path to Docker auth config.json
      --build string                   Build context directory to build before
                                       planning/applying
      --build-arg stringArray          Add a build-time variable (KEY=VALUE)
      --build-capture string           Build capture path (defaults inside --evidence-dir)
      --build-output string            Build output mode: auto, tty, or logs (default
                                       "auto")
      --build-policy string            Build policy bundle path or https URL to
                                       evaluate before/after build
      --build-policy-mode string       Build policy enforcement mode: enforce or warn
                                       (default "enforce")
      --build-secret stringArray       Expose an environment variable as a BuildKit
                                       secret (NAME)
      --builder string                 BuildKit address
      --cache-from stringArray         BuildKit cache import source
      --cache-to stringArray           BuildKit cache export destination
      --capture-tag stringArray        Tag build/apply capture sessions (KEY=VALUE).
                                       Repeatable.
      --chart string                   Chart reference (path, repo/name, or OCI ref)
      --create-namespace               Create the release namespace if it does not exist
      --dockerfile string              Path to the Dockerfile within the build context
                                       (default "Dockerfile")
      --evidence-dir string            Directory for build/apply captures, reports,
                                       and ship summary
      --explain-format string          Explain output format: text, markdown, or json
                                       (default "markdown")
      --explain-output string          Explain report path (defaults inside --evidence-dir)
      --hermetic                       Enable hermetic/locked build mode
      --load                           Load the resulting image into the local
                                       container runtime
      --locked                         Alias for --hermetic
  -n, --namespace string               Namespace for the Helm release
      --no-cache                       Disable BuildKit cache usage
      --no-capture                     Do not write build/apply capture SQLite files
      --non-interactive                Fail instead of prompting (requires --yes)
      --plan-only                      Run build, verify, and plan, then stop before apply
      --plan-output string             Plan JSON path (defaults inside --evidence-dir)
      --platform strings               Target platforms (comma-separated values like
                                       linux/amd64)
      --provenance                     Generate a SLSA provenance attestation during
                                       the build
      --push                           Push built image tags to their registries
                                       (default true)
      --release string                 Helm release name
      --s3-cache string                Enable BuildKit S3 cache import/export at
                                       s3://BUCKET[/PREFIX]
      --s3-cache-endpoint-url string   Custom S3-compatible endpoint URL for --s3-cache
      --s3-cache-mode string           S3 cache export mode: min or max
      --s3-cache-name string           S3 cache manifest name (defaults to first tag
                                       or context name)
      --s3-cache-path-style            Use path-style addressing for --s3-cache
      --s3-cache-region string         AWS region for --s3-cache
      --sandbox                        Require executing the build inside the sandbox
      --sandbox-config string          Path to a sandbox runtime config file
      --sbom                           Generate an SBOM attestation during the build
      --secret-config string           Secrets provider config file
      --secret-provider string         Secret provider name for secret:// references
      --set stringArray                Set values on the command line (key=val)
      --set-file stringArray           Set values from files (key=path)
      --set-string stringArray         Set STRING values on the command line
      --skip-build                     Skip the build step and plan/apply the chart as-is
      --skip-explain                   Skip the post-apply explain report
      --skip-verify                    Skip the verifier step and do not enforce
                                       --require-verified on apply
  -t, --tag strings                    Image tag(s) to apply to the build result
      --timeout duration               Time to wait for Kubernetes operations (default
                                       5m0s)
  -f, --values strings                 Values files to apply (can be repeated)
      --verify-fail-on string          Fail threshold for verifier findings (default
                                       "high")
      --verify-mode string             Verifier mode: warn, block, or off (default "block")
      --verify-report string           Verifier JSON report path (defaults inside
                                       --evidence-dir)
      --version string                 Chart version (default: latest)
      --wait                           Wait for resources to be ready (default true)
      --watch duration                 After a successful deploy, stream logs/events
                                       for this long
      --yes                            Skip interactive confirmation prompts
examples:
# Build an image, verify the chart, plan, apply, capture, and explain
  torque ship --chart ./chart --release api -n prod --build . --tag ghcr.io/acme/api:dev --yes

# Stop after build, verify, and PR-ready plan generation
  torque ship --chart ./chart --release api -n prod --build . --tag ghcr.io/acme/api:dev --plan-only

# Build, verify, plan, apply, capture, and explain one release
torque ship --chart ./chart --release api -n prod --build . --tag ghcr.io/acme/api:dev --yes

# Ship with a shared S3 BuildKit cache
torque ship --chart ./chart --release api -n prod --build . --tag ghcr.io/acme/api:dev --s3-cache s3://acme-build-cache/torque/main --s3-cache-region us-east-1 --yes

# Ship through a remote torque-agent gRPC endpoint
torque --remote-agent 127.0.0.1:7443 --remote-token "$TORQUE_REMOTE_TOKEN" ship --chart ./chart --release api -n prod --build . --tag ghcr.io/acme/api:dev --yes

# Ship through an mTLS-protected torque-agent
torque --remote-agent torque-agent.prod.internal:7443 --remote-token "$TORQUE_REMOTE_TOKEN" --remote-tls --remote-tls-ca /etc/torque/tls/ca.crt --remote-tls-client-cert /etc/torque/tls/client.crt --remote-tls-client-key /etc/torque/tls/client.key ship --chart ./chart --release api -n prod --build . --tag ghcr.io/acme/api:dev --yes

entries.title: torque stack
entries.subtitle: Compile and orchestrate many Helm releases as a dependency graph
entries.content:
torque stack discovers stack.yaml/release.yaml, compiles them with inheritance into a DAG, and runs torque apply/delete per release.

Minimal-flags workflow: put defaults into stack.yaml `cli:` and/or TORQUE_STACK_* env vars (see docs/stack-cli-defaults.md). Run `torque env --match stack` to discover environment overrides.

Usage:
  torque stack

Flags:
      --config string            Path to stack root directory or stack.yaml/release.yaml
      --plan-only                Compile and print the plan, but do not execute
      --profile string           Profile overlay name (defaults to
                                 stack.yaml.defaultProfile when present)
      --secret-config string     Secrets provider config file (defaults to
                                 ~/.torque/config.yaml and repo .torque.yaml)
      --secret-provider string   Secret provider name for secret:// references
examples:
# Plan the stack (default: read-only, like `torque stack plan`)
torque stack --config ./stacks/prod

# Restrict selection via environment defaults
TORQUE_STACK_TAG=critical TORQUE_STACK_CLUSTER=prod-us torque stack --config ./stacks/prod

# Emit a machine-readable plan for tooling
torque stack --config ./stacks/prod --output json

entries.title: torque stack apply
entries.subtitle: Apply the selected stack releases in DAG order
entries.content:
Apply the selected stack releases in DAG order

Usage:
  torque stack apply [flags]

Flags:
      --capture string[="__auto__"]   Capture stack run events and artifacts to a
                                      SQLite database at this path
      --capture-tag stringArray       Tag the capture session (KEY=VALUE). Repeatable.
      --from-bundle string            Run from a sealed plan bundle (.tgz)
      --helm-logs string[="on"]       Helm log capture + TTY rendering mode:
                                      off|on|all (default off)
